context.title: Attention gap: divisive vs. connecting forum posts
context.description: Coding of the 16 opening posts (page 1 of 4) of the heise-online thread 'Aktuelle Hitzewelle die schlimmste in Westeuropa seit Beginn der Aufzeichnungen' (current heatwave the worst in Western Europe since records began). Research question: how large is the engagement gap between posts that reinforce societal division and those that aim to overcome it and propose solutions? Two axes (social valence, constructive orientation) plus reply count as engagement proxy, for display in an XY scatter plot.

words.name: constructive orientation
words.scale: -1 (emphasize problem + assign blame) to +1 (propose solution + call to action)

words.name: blame assignment
words.note: has side effect on social valence (divisive) -> watch for X/Y coupling

x.rationale: Effect on social cohesion. Negative: camp-building, contempt, us-vs-them. Positive: common ground, understanding, bridge-building.

y.poles: emphasize problem + assign blame (-1) <-> propose solution + call to action (+1)
y.rationale: Negative pole unites two markers: (a) staying with the problem/affect and (b) assigning blame to others. Positive pole unites: (a) a concrete solution proposal and (b) a call to action.
y.coupling_note: The blame-assignment marker is by definition also divisive (X-). This produces a slight expected X/Y correlation: blame-assigning posts tend to fall in the lower-left quadrant. When analyzing, compute Pearson r between X and Y; if coupling is high, consider moving blame assignment onto X.

values.note: only thread on the page with no reply counter
connecting, solution-oriented hook; full text not visible

values.note: pure blame assignment, no solution/action reference; example of X/Y coupling (lower-left quadrant)

analysis_notes.hypothesis_precommit: Divisive + problem-emphasizing/blame-assigning (lower-left quadrant) correlates positively with reply count; connecting + solution/action-calling (upper-right quadrant) correlates negatively. zukunftcom 'The solution?' sits upper-right with 0 replies and supports the hypothesis as a single case.
analysis_notes.axis_coupling_precommit: Expected Pearson correlation r(X,Y) > 0 (positive), because the blame-assignment marker pulls both axes toward negative. If r > 0.7: axes carry redundant information, move blame assignment onto X and reduce Y purely to solution/complaint.
confounders: Reply count measures friction/disagreement, NOT agreement or read reach. Connecting posts can have high silent approval with few replies.
Posting time: earlier-posted threads have had more time to accumulate replies (time-on-page confounder). Consider normalizing to replies per hour.
Visibility/order in the forum affects reply count independent of content (position bias).
n=16, only page 1 of 4. No statistical significance, only hypothesis generation.
Coding by a single LLM coder, no inter-rater reliability (Cohen's kappa missing). Title-only coding without full text increases uncertainty.
next_steps: Capture full text of all posts instead of titles only.
Add a second independent coder (human or other LLM) for inter-rater reliability.
Include all 4 pages + threads from other topics as control.
If available: use reach/views as a second engagement proxy alongside replies.
